scenario_scope: Real local-project intake decision for the hashed BeastBrain historical project snapshot.

cases.decision_rationale: BeastBrain establishes primary DKL/Portia provenance and a distinct durable-memory substrate spanning typed semantic objects, ontology, graph/vector retrieval, navigation, lifecycle, and certified snapshots. The manifest action remains deferred until all old projects and independent comparators are mined.
required_before_drafting: complete remaining project mining and symbol-level provenance
ingest independent knowledge-graph, graph-RAG, learned-navigation, and agent-memory sources
write a chapter-boundary review against Virtual Context ABI, claim ledgers, and procedural memory
evidence_transition_preconditions: source note and exact claim mapping available
manifest change validated before chapter insertion
promotion_blockers: no reproduced DKL retrieval or Portia navigation result
independent external literature not yet ingested
non_claims: does not add the proposed chapter
does not claim DKL or Portia novelty, quality, or performance

cases.decision_rationale: The project provides concrete negative cases for trainable routers without parameter updates, simulated training curves, synthetic inference benchmarks, toy scoring, filtered-test overreach, tensor-layout failures, and readiness claims that outrun build/integration evidence.
required_before_drafting: map each negative case to an exact source surface
label historical versus current source state
avoid reproducing private raw logs in public prose
evidence_transition_preconditions: accepted transition required for any support-state change
non_claims: does not establish current compiler failure for every historical log
does not establish model, benchmark, hardware, security, or safety performance

validation_boundary: Records a provisional new-chapter boundary and a reviewed existing-chapter negative-evidence route. It does not validate project results, source-publication rights, or a support-state transition.
non_claims: does not publish private source material
